title: 帷幕
summary:
Captain Hastings recounts his habit of providing Hercule Poirot with detailed daily accounts to keep him connected to the outside world. During a conversation, Miss Creevy interrupts to inform Hastings that Mrs. Lestrade has woken up and wishes to see her husband. Hastings finds Lt. Col. Lestrade in a morning room, where the Colonel appears frightened and physically shaken. Hastings supports the unsteady Colonel as they go upstairs to the bedroom. Inside, the couple shares a tender, affectionate moment, which leads Hastings to feel ashamed of his previous speculation that the shooting was intentional.

At dinner, Hastings observes the guests: Mrs. Franklin appears well in a pink gown despite Dr. Franklin's troubled demeanor; Alton and Judith go to the garden after the meal; and Boyd Carlington chats with Mrs. Franklin. Elizabeth Cole remains engrossed in a book, appearing somewhat uncomfortable around Hastings. Later, Hastings finds Colonel Lestrade in Poirot's room, where the Colonel nostalgically recounts falling in love with his wife at a dance.

Hastings later discusses the incident with Poirot, admitting that both he and Boyd Carlington had suspected the shot was intentional. Poirot remains reserved but notes that things are not always as they seem and that genuine affection can exist behind animosity. As Hastings prepares for sleep, he experiences a sudden realization regarding Poirot's alert gaze. He contemplates a theory that a third party, "X," might have fired a second shot simultaneously with the Colonel's shot at a rabbit, masking the murder as an accident. He concludes that if the Colonel were to "confess" to an accidental shooting, the police might not perform the ballistic tests necessary to prove a second weapon was involved.
